SECTION_I.title: SECTION I: LIVE ANIMALS; ANIMAL PRODUCTS
SECTION_I.short_title: SECTION I: LIVE ANIMALS; ANIMAL PRODUCTS
SECTION_I.raw_notes:
1. Any reference in this Section to a particular genus or species of an animal, except where the context otherwise requires, includes a reference to the young of that genus or species.
2. Except where the context otherwise requires, throughout the Nomenclature any reference to “dried” products also covers products which have been dehydrated, evaporated or freeze-dried.

definitions.term: particular genus or species of an animal
definitions.meaning: Includes a reference to the young of that genus or species, except where the context otherwise requires.
Covers products which have been dehydrated, evaporated or freeze-dried, except where the context otherwise requires.

SECTION_II.title: SECTION II: VEGETABLE PRODUCTS
SECTION_II.raw_notes: 1. In this Section the term “pellets” means products which have been agglomerated either directly by compression or by the addition of a binder in a proportion not exceeding 3% by weight.

definitions.meaning: products which have been agglomerated either directly by compression or by the addition of a binder in a proportion not exceeding 3% by weight

SECTION_III.title: SECTION III: ANIMAL, VEGETABLE OR MICROBIAL FATS AND OILS AND THEIR CLEAVAGE PRODUCTS; PREPARED EDIBLE FATS; ANIMAL OR VEGETABLE WAXES

SECTION_IV.title: SECTION IV: PREPARED FOODSTUFFS; BEVERAGES, SPIRITS AND VINEGAR; TOBACCO AND MANUFACTURED TOBACCO SUBSTITUTES; PRODUCTS, WHETHER OR NOT CONTAINING NICOTINE, INTENDED FOR INHALATION WITHOUT COMBUSTION; OTHER NICOTINE CONTAINING PRODUCTS INTENDED FOR THE INTAKE OF NICOTINE INTO THE HUMAN BODY
SECTION_IV.short_title: SECTION IV: PREPARED FOODSTUFFS; BEVERAGES, SPIRITS AND VINEGAR; TOBACCO AND NICOTINE PRODUCTS
SECTION_IV.raw_notes: 1. In this Section the term “pellets” means products which have been agglomerated either directly by compression or by the addition of a binder in a proportion not exceeding 3% by weight.

definitions.meaning: products which have been agglomerated either directly by compression or by the addition of a binder in a proportion not exceeding 3% by weight

SECTION_VI.title: SECTION VI: PRODUCTS OF THE CHEMICAL OR ALLIED INDUSTRIES
SECTION_VI.short_title: SECTION VI: PRODUCTS OF THE CHEMICAL INDUSTRIES
SECTION_VI.raw_notes:
1. (A) Goods (other than radioactive ores) answering to a description in heading 28.44 or 28.45 are to be classified in those headings and in no other heading of the Nomenclature.
(B) Subject to paragraph (A) above, goods answering to a description in heading 28.43, 28.46 or 28.52 are to be classified in those headings and in no other heading of this Section.
2. Subject to Note 1 above, goods classifiable in heading 30.04, 30.05, 30.06, 32.12, 33.03, 33.04, 33.05, 33.06, 33.07, 35.06, 37.07 or 38.08 by reason of being put up in measured doses or for retail sale are to be classified in those headings and in no other heading of the Nomenclature.
3. Goods put up in sets consisting of two or more separate constituents, some or all of which fall in this Section and are intended to be mixed together to obtain a product of Section VI or VII, are to be classified in the heading appropriate to that product, provided that the constituents are:
(a) having regard to the manner in which they are put up, clearly identifiable as being intended to be used together without first being repacked;
(b) presented together; and
(c) identifiable, whether by their nature or by the relative proportions in which they are present, as being complementary one to another.
4. Where a product answers to a description in one or more of the headings in Section VI by virtue of being described by name or function and also to heading 38.27, then it is classifiable in a heading that references the product by name or function and not under heading 38.27.

exclusions.condition: Goods (other than radioactive ores) answering to a description in heading 28.44 or 28.45
exclusions.action: Classified in those headings and in no other heading of the Nomenclature

exclusions.condition: Goods answering to a description in heading 28.43, 28.46 or 28.52 (subject to the above exclusion for 28.44/28.45)
exclusions.action: Classified in those headings and in no other heading of this Section

exclusions.condition: Goods classifiable in heading 30.04, 30.05, 30.06, 32.12, 33.03, 33.04, 33.05, 33.06, 33.07, 35.06, 37.07 or 38.08 by reason of being put up in measured doses or for retail sale
exclusions.action: Classified in those headings and in no other heading of the Nomenclature

exclusions.condition: Product answers to a description in one or more headings in Section VI by name or function and also to heading 38.27
exclusions.action: Classified in a heading that references the product by name or function and not under heading 38.27

classification_rules.rule: Goods put up in sets consisting of two or more separate constituents, some or all of which fall in this Section and are intended to be mixed together to obtain a product of Section VI or VII, are to be classified in the heading appropriate to that product, provided the constituents are: (a) clearly identifiable as being intended to be used together without first being repacked; (b) presented together; and (c) identifiable as being complementary one to another by their nature or relative proportions.

SECTION_VII.title: SECTION VII: PLASTICS AND ARTICLES THEREOF; RUBBER AND ARTICLES THEREOF
SECTION_VII.short_title: SECTION VII: PLASTICS, RUBBER AND ARTICLES THEREOF
SECTION_VII.raw_notes:
1. Goods put up in sets consisting of two or more separate constituents, some or all of which fall in this Section and are intended to be mixed together to obtain a product of Section VI or VII, are to be classified in the heading appropriate to that product, provided that the constituents are:
(a) having regard to the manner in which they are put up, clearly identifiable as being intended to be used together without first being repacked;
(b) presented together; and
(c) identifiable, whether by their nature or by the relative proportions in which they are present, as being complementary one to another.
2. Except for the goods of heading 39.18 or 39.19, plastics, rubber, and articles thereof, printed with motifs, characters or pictorial representations, which are not merely subsidiary to the primary use of the goods, fall in Chapter 49.

exclusions.condition: Plastics, rubber, and articles thereof, printed with motifs, characters or pictorial representations, which are not merely subsidiary to the primary use of the goods

classification_rules.rule: Goods put up in sets consisting of two or more separate constituents, some or all of which fall in this Section and are intended to be mixed together to obtain a product of Section VI or VII, are to be classified in the heading appropriate to that product, provided that the constituents are: (a) clearly identifiable as being intended to be used together without first being repacked; (b) presented together; and (c) identifiable, whether by their nature or by the relative proportions, as being complementary one to another.

Articles of Chapter 97

definitions.term: put up for retail sale (for yarn in Chapters 50, 51, 52, 54, 55)
definitions.meaning: Yarn (single, multiple, cabled) put up: (a) on cards/reels/tubes etc., weight (incl. support) ≤85g for silk/waste silk/man-made filament, ≤125g others; (b) in balls/hanks/skeins weight ≤85g for man-made filament <3000 dtex/silk, ≤125g for other yarns <2000 dtex, ≤500g others; (c) in hanks/skeins with dividing threads, each uniform weight ≤85g for silk/waste silk/man-made filament, ≤125g others. Subject to exceptions in Note 4(B).
Multiple (folded) or cabled yarn: (a) put up on supports (e.g., reels, tubes) weight (incl. support) ≤1000g; (b) dressed for use as sewing thread; (c) with a final 'Z' twist.

Yarn having tenacity > specified values: Single yarn of nylon/polyamides/polyesters >60 cN/tex; Multiple/cabled yarn of nylon/polyamides/polyesters >53 cN/tex; Single/multiple/cabled yarn of viscose rayon >27 cN/tex.

Textile products that are: (a) cut otherwise than into squares/rectangles; (b) produced finished, ready for use (or merely needing separation by cutting dividing threads); (c) cut to size with at least one heat-sealed tapered/compressed edge and other edges treated; (d) hemmed, rolled, or knotted fringe edges (excluding simply whipped edges); (e) cut to size with drawn thread work; (f) assembled by sewing/gumming etc. (excluding joined piece goods or layered textiles); (g) knitted/crocheted to shape.
Includes 'dipped'.
Includes 'aramids'.

Filament yarn (incl. monofilament) of synthetic textile material (other than textured yarn) which does not break on extension to 3x original length and returns, after extension to 2x original length within 5 min, to ≤1.5x original length.
Garments of headings 61.01 to 61.14 and 62.01 to 62.11.

Yarn with natural fibre colour, not bleached/dyed/printed; or indeterminate colour ('grey yarn') from garneted stock. May have colourless dressing/fugitive dye; man-made fibres may have delustring agents.

Yarn that: (i) is bleached, made of bleached fibres, dyed white, or treated with white dressing; (ii) is a mixture of unbleached and bleached fibres; (iii) is multiple/cabled and consists of unbleached and bleached yarns.

Yarn that: (i) is dyed (not white/fugitive) or printed, or made from dyed/printed fibres; (ii) is a mixture of dyed fibres of different colours or unbleached/bleached with coloured fibres (marl/mixture), or printed at intervals; (iii) is from printed slivers/rovings; (iv) is multiple/cabled and consists of unbleached/bleached and coloured yarn.
Woven fabric from unbleached yarn, not bleached/dyed/printed. May have colourless dressing/fugitive dye.
Woven fabric that: (i) is bleached, dyed white, or treated with white dressing in the piece; (ii) consists of bleached yarn; (iii) consists of unbleached and bleached yarn.
Woven fabric that: (i) is dyed a single uniform colour (not white) or treated with coloured finish (not white) in the piece; (ii) consists of coloured yarn of a single uniform colour.

definitions.term: woven fabric of yarns of different colours
definitions.meaning: Woven fabric (not printed) that: (i) consists of yarns of different colours/shades (not natural fibre colour); (ii) consists of unbleached/bleached and coloured yarn; (iii) consists of marl/mixture yarns. (Selvedges/piece ends not considered.)
Woven fabric printed in the piece, whether or not from yarns of different colours. Includes designs made by brush/spray/transfer paper/flocking/batik.
Fabric construction where each weft yarn passes alternately over/under successive warp yarns and each warp yarn passes alternately over/under successive weft yarns.

classification_rules.rule: For goods classifiable in Chapters 50 to 55 or heading 58.09 or 59.02 of a mixture of textile materials: classify as if consisting wholly of the one textile material that predominates by weight. If no single material predominates by weight, classify as if consisting wholly of the material covered by the heading which occurs last in numerical order among those which equally merit consideration.

For the mixture rule: (a) Gimped horsehair yarn (51.10) and metallised yarn (56.05) are treated as a single textile material (weight = aggregate). For woven fabrics, metal thread is regarded as a textile material. (b) Determine Chapter first, then heading within that Chapter, disregarding materials not in that Chapter. (c) When Chapters 54 and 55 are involved with any other Chapter, treat 54 and 55 as a single Chapter. (d) Where a Chapter/heading refers to goods of different textile materials, treat those materials as a single textile material.
The mixture rules (Note 2(A) and (B)) also apply to yarns referred to in Notes 3, 4, 5, or 6.

For purposes of Section XI, certain yarns are treated as 'twine, cordage, ropes and cables' if they meet specified descriptions (e.g., silk >20,000 dtex, man-made fibres >10,000 dtex, true hemp/flax polished/glazed ≥1,429 dtex or not polished/glazed >20,000 dtex, coir of ≥3 plies, other veg fibres >20,000 dtex, or reinforced with metal thread). Exceptions listed in Note 3(B).
Chapters 50 to 55 and 60, and (except where context otherwise requires) Chapters 56 to 59, do not apply to goods 'made up' within meaning of Note 7.
Chapters 50 to 55 and 60 do not apply to goods of Chapters 56 to 59.
Woven fabrics of Chapters 50 to 55 include fabrics consisting of layers of parallel yarns superimposed at acute/right angles, bonded at intersections by adhesive or thermal bonding.
Elastic products consisting of textile materials combined with rubber threads are classified in Section XI.
Unless context otherwise requires, textile garments (headings 61.01-61.14, 62.01-62.11) of different headings are classified in their own headings even if put up in sets for retail sale.

Subject to Note 1 to Section XI, textiles, garments and other textile articles incorporating chemical/mechanical/electronic components for additional functionality (whether built-in or within fibre/fabric) are classified in their respective headings in Section XI provided they retain the essential character of goods of this Section.

For products of Chapters 56 to 63 containing two or more textile materials: regard as consisting wholly of that textile material which would be selected under Note 2 for classification of a product of Chapters 50-55 or heading 58.09 consisting of the same textile materials.

For applying the rule for Chapters 56-63: (a) where appropriate, only the part determining classification under Interpretative Rule 3 is taken into account; (b) for products with ground fabric and pile/looped surface, no account is taken of ground fabric; (c) for embroidery of heading 58.10 and goods thereof, only ground fabric is taken into account (but embroidery without visible ground is classified with reference to embroidering threads alone).

definitions.meaning: Articles of heading 73.07, 73.12, 73.15, 73.17 or 73.18 and similar articles of other base metal (excluding articles for medical implants in heading 90.21); springs and leaves for springs, of base metal (excluding clock or watch springs in heading 91.14); articles of headings 83.01, 83.02, 83.08, 83.10 and frames and mirrors, of base metal, of heading 83.06.

Iron and steel, copper, nickel, aluminium, lead, zinc, tin, tungsten (wolfram), molybdenum, tantalum, magnesium, cobalt, bismuth, cadmium, titanium, zirconium, antimony, manganese, beryllium, chromium, germanium, vanadium, gallium, hafnium, indium, niobium (columbium), rhenium and thallium.
Products containing a microscopic heterogeneous combination of a metallic component and a ceramic component. Includes sintered metal carbides (metal carbides sintered with a metal).
Includes sintered mixtures of metal powders, heterogeneous intimate mixtures obtained by melting (other than cermets) and intermetallic compounds.
All metal waste and scrap; metal goods definitely not usable as such because of breakage, cutting-up, wear or other reasons.
Products of which 90% or more by weight passes through a sieve having a mesh aperture of 1 mm.

Rolled, extruded, drawn or forged products, not in coils, with a uniform solid cross-section along their whole length in specific shapes (circles, ovals, rectangles, equilateral triangles, regular convex polygons). Thickness of rectangular cross-section exceeds one-tenth of the width. Includes cast or sintered products subsequently worked (not assuming character of other articles).

Rolled, extruded, drawn, forged or formed products, coiled or not, of a uniform cross-section along their whole length, which do not conform to definitions of bars, rods, wire, plates, sheets, strip, foil, tubes or pipes. Includes cast or sintered products subsequently worked (not assuming character of other articles).

Rolled, extruded or drawn products, in coils, with a uniform solid cross-section along their whole length in specific shapes (circles, ovals, rectangles, equilateral triangles, regular convex polygons). Thickness of rectangular cross-section exceeds one-tenth of the width.

Flat-surfaced products (other than unwrought), coiled or not, of solid rectangular (other than square) cross-section with a uniform thickness. Rectangular shape: thickness not exceeding one-tenth of the width. Other shapes: any size, provided they do not assume character of other articles. Includes patterned, perforated, corrugated, polished or coated products if not assuming character of other articles.

Hollow products, coiled or not, with a uniform cross-section with only one enclosed void along their whole length in specific shapes (circles, ovals, rectangles, equilateral triangles, regular convex polygons), and a uniform wall thickness. Includes products with polished, coated, bent, threaded, drilled, waisted, expanded, cone-shaped or fitted with flanges, collars or rings.

classification_rules.rule: In Chapters 73 to 76 and 78 to 82 (but not in heading 73.15), references to parts of goods do not include references to parts of general use.
Subject to the preceding paragraph and to Note 1 to Chapter 83, the articles of Chapter 82 or 83 are excluded from Chapters 72 to 76 and 78 to 81.

Classification of alloys (other than ferro-alloys and master alloys as defined in Chapters 72 and 74): (a) An alloy of base metals is classified as an alloy of the metal which predominates by weight over each of the other metals. (b) An alloy composed of base metals of this Section and of elements not falling within this Section is treated as an alloy of base metals of this Section if the total weight of such metals equals or exceeds the total weight of the other elements present.
Unless the context otherwise requires, any reference to a base metal includes a reference to alloys which, by virtue of Note 5, are to be classified as alloys of that metal.

Classification of composite articles: Except where the headings otherwise require, articles of base metal (including articles of mixed materials treated as articles of base metal under the General Interpretative Rules) containing two or more base metals are treated as articles of the base metal predominating by weight over each of the other metals. For this purpose: (a) Iron and steel, or different kinds of iron or steel, are regarded as one and the same metal; (b) An alloy is regarded as being entirely composed of that metal as an alloy of which, by virtue of Note 5, it is classified; and (c) A cermet of heading 81.13 is regarded as a single base metal.

definitions.meaning: Any machine, machinery, plant, equipment, apparatus or appliance cited in the headings of Chapter 84 or 85.

definitions.term: electrical and electronic waste and scrap
definitions.meaning: Electrical and electronic assemblies, printed circuit boards, and electrical or electronic articles that: (i) have been rendered unusable for their original purposes by breakage, cutting-up or other processes or are economically unsuitable for repair, refurbishment or renovation to render them fit for their original purposes; and (ii) are packaged or shipped in a manner not intended to protect individual articles from damage during transportation, loading and unloading operations.

classification_rules.rule: Subject to Note 1 and the chapter notes, parts of machines (not being parts of articles of heading 84.84, 85.44, 85.45, 85.46 or 85.47) are classified as follows: (a) Parts included in any heading of Chapter 84 or 85 (except specified exclusions) are classified in their respective headings. (b) Other parts suitable for use solely/principally with a particular kind of machine or machines of the same heading are classified with those machines or in specified residual headings (e.g., 84.09, 85.03). Parts equally suitable for use principally with goods of headings 85.17 and 85.25-85.28 go to 85.17. Parts suitable for use solely/principally with goods of heading 85.24 go to 85.29. (c) All other parts are classified in specified residual headings (e.g., 84.09, 85.03) or, failing that, in heading 84.87 or 85.48.

Composite machines consisting of two or more machines fitted together to form a whole, and machines designed to perform two or more complementary or alternative functions, are classified as if consisting only of the component or being the machine which performs the principal function.

Where a machine (including a combination) consists of individual components intended to contribute together to a clearly defined function covered by one heading in Chapter 84 or 85, the whole is classified in the heading appropriate to that function.
Mixed consignments of 'electrical and electronic waste and scrap' and other waste and scrap are to be classified in heading 85.49.
